name: Liérganes, Río Miera, Portillo de la Sia, Asón, Alto de Alisas – circular through the Parque Natural de los Collados de Asón
name_original: Liérganes, Río Miera, Portillo de la Sia, Asón, Alto de Alisas – circular por el Parque Natural de los Collados de Asón
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Lic-Rio Miera traverse a landscape characterized by varied topography, from high mountain terrain to wider river valleys. The Miera River carves through limestone formations, creating narrow gorges and dramatic karst features. The region features steep relief, extensive beech and holm oak forests, and well-preserved riparian areas along the riverbanks. This diverse environment provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Lic-Rio Miera The most popular road cycling route is Liérganes, Río Miera, Portillo de la Sia, Asón, Alto de Alisas – circular through the Parque Natural de los Collados de Asón, a 59.9 miles (96.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 42 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation changes through a natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villaverde de Pontones, Ajo, Convento de San Ildefonso – circular route, a moderate 27.1 miles (43.7 km) path. This route explores the coastal areas and inland villages, offering varied scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Portillo Lunada – La Sía Pass loop from Liérganes, a 69.9 miles (112.5 km) trail leading through mountainous passes, often completed in about 6 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Lic-Rio Miera is defined by steep relief, limestone outcrops, and lush forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley rides to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Lic-Rio Miera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 1900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lic-Rio Miera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Lic-Río Miera region?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes available in the Lic-Río Miera region, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes, with a few easier options as well.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Lic-Río Miera?
A: The Lic-Río Miera region offers a diverse and challenging terrain for road cycling. You can expect significant elevation changes, particularly in the mountainous upper sections of the Miera Valley, with steep climbs and descents. The landscape transitions from high mountain terrain with limestone outcrops and karst formations to wider valleys with meadows and fields closer to the coast. The region is known for its steep relief, making it ideal for demanding rides, but also offers more rolling or flatter sections in the wider valleys.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Lic-Río Miera?
A: Road cycling routes in Lic-Río Miera range from moderate to difficult. Out of 43 routes, 23 are classified as moderate and 19 as difficult, with a few easier options. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004554678/lierganes-rio-miera-portillo-de-la-sia-ason-alto-de-alisas-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-los-collados-de-ason" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liérganes, Río Miera, Portillo de la Sia, Asón, Alto de Alisas – circular through the Parque Natural de los Collados de Asón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20166397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Lunada – La Sía Pass loop from Liérganes&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, making them suitable for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Lic-Río Miera region?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Lic-Río Miera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004554678/lierganes-rio-miera-portillo-de-la-sia-ason-alto-de-alisas-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-los-collados-de-ason" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liérganes, Río Miera, Portillo de la Sia, Asón, Alto de Alisas – circular through the Parque Natural de los Collados de Asón&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005126844/villaverde-de-pontones-ajo-convento-de-san-ildefonso-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaverde de Pontones, Ajo, Convento de San Ildefonso – circular route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Lic-Río Miera?
A: Road cycling routes in Lic-Río Miera offer stunning natural beauty and historical points of interest. You can encounter dramatic karst landscapes, lush beech and holm oak forests, and the ecologically significant Miera River. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguanaz River and Frenchman’s Cave&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos III Gate&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4051130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Miera-Rubalcaba&lt;/a&gt;. The region's varied topography, from glacial morphology in the upper valley to wider river plains, provides a constantly changing scenic backdrop.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Lic-Río Miera?
A: The road cycling experience in Lic-Río Miera is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning natural landscapes including the Miera River valley and its forests, and the rewarding climbs through mountainous passes. Over 1900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore this area, highlighting its appeal.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Lic-Río Miera?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not always explicitly marked, you can generally find parking in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for routes, such as Liérganes, Villaverde de Pontones, or La Cavada. It's advisable to check local regulations or look for public parking facilities upon arrival.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Lic-Río Miera using public transport?
A: Accessing road cycling routes via public transport in the Lic-Río Miera region can be possible, especially to larger towns like Liérganes. However, public transport options might be limited, particularly for reaching more remote trailheads in the mountainous areas. It's recommended to research local bus or train schedules in advance and confirm if bicycles are permitted on board.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Lic-Río Miera?
A: While many routes in Lic-Río Miera feature significant elevation changes and are rated moderate to difficult, there are some easier sections, particularly in the wider valley areas around towns like Liérganes. For families, look for routes with less elevation gain or consider shorter, flatter segments of longer routes. The region's varied topography means that while challenging climbs are prevalent, some gentler options exist.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes in Lic-Río Miera?
A: Yes, you will find cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the towns and villages scattered throughout the Lic-Río Miera region, such as Liérganes, Villaverde de Pontones, and La Cavada. These establishments offer great opportunities to refuel and rest during your ride, especially after tackling some of the more challenging climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Lic-Río Miera?
A: The best time for road cycling in Lic-Río Miera is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. These seasons offer more favorable weather conditions, with milder temperatures and less precipitation. The mountainous passes can be more challenging or even inaccessible in winter due to snow and ice, so planning your trip outside of winter is recommended for the most enjoyable experience.

Q: Is the Lic-Río Miera region known for professional cycling?
A: Yes, the Lic-Río Miera region has a strong connection to professional cycling. It is home to the women's road bicycle racing team, 'Río Miera–Cantabria Deporte,' which participates in elite women's races. This highlights the region's reputation and suitability for challenging road cycling, offering terrain that is ideal for both training and competition.

Q: What makes the Miera River valley ecologically significant?
A: The Miera River valley is ecologically significant due to its designation as a Special Zone of Conservation (ZEC) and a Site of Community Importance (LIC). This protection highlights its high ecological value, particularly its well-preserved riparian areas with alder and willow forests, and its role as a natural corridor. The river's course through varied landscapes, from steep gorges to wider valleys, supports a diverse ecosystem.
tips: Covalruyo viewpoint. When you reach this curve of the Portillo de Lunada you will see a sign with this name next to some stairs that lead to the viewpoint. The view from there is impressive.

A small mountain pass in the easternmost sector of the Cordillera. At 1,316 meters above sea level, it is the third highest mountain pass in Cantabria. The port is closed very frequently in winter, especially in its Cantabrian part, being closed permanently for several months on many occasions.

The Collado de Asón is an 11km section with a drop of 530m and an average gradient of 4.81%. The asphalt is perfect, with wooden fences on the curves, horizontal marking on the margins, etc. The traffic is light.
Mythical port in Cantabria with views that will surprise you. It is very enjoyable by bike, the road is in good condition and everything is well signposted. You go through very pretty towns.
